page: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-300s-amd is an AMD-based virtual server belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 300 GB NVMe SSD. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor includes hardware support for AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and FMA instructions.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing this instance in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance under stress-ng and PassMark CPU testing falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording an average of 118.62 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 68.98 GB/sec for uncached writes. Single-threaded gzip compression performs strongly, reaching 30.38 GB/sec. However, resource-intensive tasks such as medium-sized LLM prompt processing show weaker performance, averaging 25.18 tokens/sec.

This server offers a cost-effective option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained peak loads, which is a notable tradeoff for production environments. However, the inclusion of 300 GB of fast NVMe storage and a balanced 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for web serving, database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-300s-amd is an AMD EPYC-Rome virtual server within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, configured with 8 shared vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff in performance consistency under sustained loads, the server remains a highly cost-efficient solution for compute-leaning workloads. It is well-suited for static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-300s-amd is an AMD EPYC virtual server featuring 8 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 300 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving and databases.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-300s-amd features 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 300 GB NVMe SSD storage for balanced, cost-efficient compute workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual server featuring eight shared cores and local NVMe storage for balanced, compute-leaning workloads.

300 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Average multi-core execution and memory bandwidth capabilities
Suitable for static web serving, database hosting, and general-purpose applications
